summary: I read the ask, the codebase-explorer discovery notes, and the clarifying-questions writer's open questions (there were no resolved inputs in `input.json`), then wrote 23 atomic business requirements to `.pipeliner/phases/01-define/main/requirements-writer/output/requirements.md`. Each is phrased as "When X happens, Y should happen" in plain non-technical language and grouped into pausing behavior (R1–R6), the five-item paused menu — Explore, Clarifying Questions, Ask Human, Repeat from the Beginning, Done (R7–R14), manual one-action-at-a-time stepping (R15–R17), safeguards around the automatic loop and retry cap (R18–R21), and non-technical integrity plus preserving existing gate behavior (R22–R23). I closed with a short list of the assumed open-question answers each requirement rests on, so a human can see what would change if they override a default.
